title: This, that, that-over-there
objectives: Point at things at different distances using demonstratives
Match demonstratives in gender and number
grammar_notes: Spanish has THREE distance levels (here / there-by-you / over-there), unlike English's two.
Russian uses 'этот / тот' (two levels) — Spanish 'aquel' adds a third.
Neuter forms (esto, eso, aquello) refer to ideas or unidentified objects: '¿Qué es esto?'
example_phrases: Este libro es mío. (This book is mine.)
Esa mesa es grande. (That table is big.)
